parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: In your English class you have been talking about how schools test their students. Now your English teacher has asked you to write an essay. Write an essay using all the notes and giving reasons for your point of view.

stimulus.body:
"Final exams are the fairest way to judge what a student has learned. Do you agree?"

Notes

Write about:
1. stress
2. equal conditions for everyone
3. ............... (your own idea)

bullets: give reasons for your point of view

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: You have received this letter from your English-speaking friend Nat.

stimulus.body:
My final exams start in six weeks and I'm already panicking. Every time I sit down to revise, I end up staring at my notes without taking anything in. You always seem so calm before exams — what's your secret? How do you organise your revision? And what do you do the night before a big exam?
Wish me luck,
Nat

bullets: what to do the night before an exam

tasks.lead: Your school principal is considering replacing some final exams with project work and has asked you to write a report.

stimulus.title: Report: exams or project work?
stimulus.body: Describe how students at your school feel about final exams, explain the advantages and disadvantages of replacing some of them with project work, and make a recommendation to the principal.

bullets: how students feel about final exams
advantages and disadvantages of project work

tasks.lead: You see this announcement on an English-language website for students.

stimulus.title: Revision tools — reviewed by you
stimulus.body: Flashcard apps, video channels, study planners — students are surrounded by revision tools that promise better grades. Write a review of one you have used, describing how it works, saying how it affected your results, and explaining who would benefit from it most.

bullets: who would benefit from it most
